description: Fix SDK reference documentation: httpStatusToToolError description and missing prompt name validation regex
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Document 5xx retryability and 503 Retry-After parsing in httpStatusToToolError
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want to understand which HTTP errors are automatically retryable so that I don't add redundant retry logic
acceptanceCriteria: sdk/utilities.mdx httpStatusToToolError description mentions that 5xx errors are mapped to internal with retryable: true
sdk/utilities.mdx httpStatusToToolError description mentions that 503 specifically parses the Retry-After header and includes retryAfterMs
The status code mapping table or description distinguishes between 5xx (retryable) and other non-categorized codes (not retryable)
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx line 435 describes httpStatusToToolError as: 'Automatically classifies status codes: 401/403 -> auth, 404 -> not_found, 429 -> rate_limit (with Retry-After header parsing), everything else -> internal.'

The actual implementation in platform/plugin-sdk/src/fetch.ts lines 27-34 has important distinctions:
- Status >= 500: category 'internal' with `retryable: true`
- Status 503 specifically: also parses Retry-After header and includes retryAfterMs
- Non-5xx non-categorized: category 'internal' without retryable: true

Update the description to reflect these distinctions. A table or bullet list would work well:
- 401/403 → auth
- 404 → not_found
- 429 → rate_limit (parses Retry-After)
- 503 → internal, retryable: true (parses Retry-After)
- 5xx (other) → internal, retryable: true
- Other → internal

userStories.title: Add prompt name validation regex to prompts SDK reference
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want to know the prompt naming rules upfront so that my build doesn't fail with a validation error
acceptanceCriteria: sdk/prompts.mdx documents the prompt name validation regex: ^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9_-]*$
The naming rules are presented similarly to how the tools page documents its naming regex
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
docs/content/docs/sdk/prompts.mdx line 60 only says prompt names 'must be non-empty'. But the build command validates names against ^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9_-]*$ — see platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts line 316.

The tools page (docs/content/docs/sdk/tools.mdx) documents its naming regex as ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*(_[a-z0-9]+)*$. The prompts page should follow the same pattern: add a naming conventions section near where it says 'must be non-empty' that shows the regex and explains the rules (start with lowercase letter or digit, can contain lowercase letters, digits, underscores, and hyphens).
